chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT MIRPURKHAS Constitutional Petition No.S-180 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 16.03.2026 Mr. Ghulam Nabi Meo Rajput advocate for the petitioner alongwith petitioner. Mr. Vijesh Kumar advocate for the respondent No.1 a/w respondent No.1. Mr. Ayaz Ali Rajpar, A.A.G. = Heard both the parties and their respective counsel(s). After arguing the matter at length, both the learned counsel(s) state, under instructions of their respective client, that they would be satisfied if the instant Petition is disposed of in the following terms:- 1. With respect to the meeting of the minor with father, it is stated by both the parties that same are being conducted as per schedule drawn by the learned trial Court and they have no any issue with the same. 2. With respect to the claim of the maintenance of the minor, which was allowed by the learned Appellate Court from institution of the suit instead of 08-12-2020 (which according to the petitioner was the date when she was allegedly ousted from the house of the respondent); both the parties state that Decree be modified and the date, from which maintenance of the minor should start from 08-12-2020. 3. With respect to the maintenance of the minor starting from 08-12-2020 till August, 2023, the Respondent states that he will deposit the amount within a period of three months positively. 4. With respect to the claim of medical expenses of Rs. 150,000/= by the petitioner, the petitioner claims that Rs.70,000/= were paid in cash by the petitioner’s father to the respondent, such fact is denied by the Respondent in Court today.

With respect to the maintenance of the minor starting from 08-12-2020 till August, 2023, the Respondent states that he will deposit the amount within a period of three months positively. 4. With respect to the claim of medical expenses of Rs. 150,000/= by the petitioner, the petitioner claims that Rs.70,000/= were paid in cash by the petitioner’s father to the respondent, such fact is denied by the Respondent in Court today. Receipts of payments for medical filed by the Respondent were also seen. Based on the above, the petitioner, her father and her counsel state that they are 2 satisfied and there is no further claim in respect of the said item, which stands relinquished by the petitioner. 5. With respect to the quantum of maintenance of the petitioner, which was reduced from Rs.10,000/= to Rs.5,000/= by the learned Appellate Court, both the parties state that they are satisfied with the reduced quantum. 6. Both the learned counsel state that Execution Application has already been allowed as per Order dated 05-03-2026. Respondent undertakes to deposit the balance amount of Rs.71,000/= on or before 05-04-2026. Accordingly, the above terms and undertakings are made part of the Order of the Court. The instant Petition is, therefore, disposed of, by consent of the parties, in above terms and the Decree(s) impugned are modified to the above extent accordingly. JUDGE “Saleem”
